235	/**
236	 * Text classification pipeline using any `ModelForSequenceClassification`.
237	 *
238	 * **Example:** Sentiment-analysis w/ `Xenova/distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english`.
239	 * ```javascript
240	 * const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis', 'Xenova/distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english');
241	 * const output = await classifier('I love transformers!');
242	 * // [{ label: 'POSITIVE', score: 0.999788761138916 }]
243	 * ```
244	 *
245	 * **Example:** Multilingual sentiment-analysis w/ `Xenova/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment` (and return top 5 classes).
246	 * ```javascript
247	 * const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis', 'Xenova/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment');
248	 * const output = await classifier('Le meilleur film de tous les temps.', { top_k: 5 });
249	 * // [

251	 * //   { label: '4 stars', score: 0.03323351591825485 },
252	 * //   { label: '3 stars', score: 0.0036155181005597115 },
253	 * //   { label: '1 star', score: 0.0011325967498123646 },
254	 * //   { label: '2 stars', score: 0.0009423971059732139 }
255	 * // ]
256	 * ```
257	 *
258	 * **Example:** Toxic comment classification w/ `Xenova/toxic-bert` (and return all classes).

260	 * const classifier = await pipeline('text-classification', 'Xenova/toxic-bert');
261	 * const output = await classifier('I hate you!', { top_k: null });
262	 * // [
263	 * //   { label: 'toxic', score: 0.9593140482902527 },
264	 * //   { label: 'insult', score: 0.16187334060668945 },

267	 * //   { label: 'threat', score: 0.019197041168808937 },
268	 * //   { label: 'severe_toxic', score: 0.005651099607348442 }
269	 * // ]
270	 * ```
271	 */

1975	export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

1978	     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.

1981	    constructor(options) {
1982	        super(options);
1983	    }

1985	    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
1986	    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {
1987	
1988	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
1989	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
1990	
1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
1992	
1993	        const toReturn = [];
1994	        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
1995	            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
1996	            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
1997	            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
1998	                skip_special_tokens: true,
1999	            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
2000	            toReturn.push(decoded);
2001	        }
2002	
2003	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
2004	    }
2005	}

2742	    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
2743	    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {
2744	
2745	        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1
2746	
2747	        // Preprocess image
2748	        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
2749	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);
2750	
2751	        // Run tokenization
2752	        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
2753	        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
2754	            add_special_tokens: false,
2755	            padding: true,
2756	            truncation: true,
2757	        }).input_ids;
2758	
2759	        // Run model
2760	        const output = await this.model.generate({
2761	            inputs: pixel_values,
2762	            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
2763	            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
2764	            decoder_input_ids,
2765	            ...generate_kwargs,
2766	        });
2767	
2768	        // Decode output
2769	        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];
2770	
2771	        // Parse answer
2772	        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
2773	        let answer = null;
2774	        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
2775	            answer = match[1].trim();
2776	        }
2777	        return [{ answer }];
2778	    }
2779	}

135	/**
136	 * Helper function to convert list [xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax] into object { "xmin": xmin, ... }
137	 * @param {number[]} box The bounding box as a list.
138	 * @param {boolean} asInteger Whether to cast to integers.
139	 * @returns {BoundingBox} The bounding box as an object.
140	 * @private
141	 */
142	function get_bounding_box(box, asInteger) {
143	    if (asInteger) {
144	        box = box.map(x => x | 0);
145	    }
146	    const [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] = box;
147	
148	    return { xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax };

138	 * @param {boolean} asInteger Whether to cast to integers.

142	function get_bounding_box(box, asInteger) {
143	    if (asInteger) {
144	        box = box.map(x => x | 0);

1975	export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
1976	
1977	    /**
1978	     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
1979	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
1980	     */
1981	    constructor(options) {
1982	        super(options);
1983	    }
1984	
1985	    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
1986	    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {
1987	
1988	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
1989	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
1990	
1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
1992	
1993	        const toReturn = [];
1994	        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
1995	            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
1996	            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
1997	            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
1998	                skip_special_tokens: true,
1999	            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
2000	            toReturn.push(decoded);
2001	        }
2002	
2003	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
2004	    }
2005	}

2732	export class DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
2733	
2734	    /**
2735	     * Create a new DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline.
2736	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
2737	     */
2738	    constructor(options) {
2739	        super(options);
2740	    }
2741	
2742	    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
2743	    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {
2744	
2745	        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1
2746	
2747	        // Preprocess image
2748	        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
2749	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);
2750	
2751	        // Run tokenization
2752	        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
2753	        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
2754	            add_special_tokens: false,
2755	            padding: true,
2756	            truncation: true,
2757	        }).input_ids;
2758	
2759	        // Run model
2760	        const output = await this.model.generate({
2761	            inputs: pixel_values,
2762	            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
2763	            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
2764	            decoder_input_ids,
2765	            ...generate_kwargs,
2766	        });
2767	
2768	        // Decode output
2769	        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];
2770	
2771	        // Parse answer
2772	        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
2773	        let answer = null;
2774	        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
2775	            answer = match[1].trim();
2776	        }
2777	        return [{ answer }];
2778	    }
2779	}

1	# The `pipeline` API
2	
3	Just like the [transformers Python library](https://github.com/huggingface/transformers), Transformers.js provides users with a simple way to leverage the power of transformers. The `pipeline()` function is the easiest and fastest way to use a pretrained model for inference. 
4	
5	<Tip>
6	
7	For the full list of available tasks/pipelines, check out [this table](#available-tasks).
8	
9	</Tip>
10	
11	
12	## The basics
13	
14	Start by creating an instance of `pipeline()` and specifying a task you want to use it for. For example, to create a sentiment analysis pipeline, you can do:
15	
16	```javascript
17	import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers';
18	
19	const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis');
20	```
21	
22	When running for the first time, the `pipeline` will download and cache the default pretrained model associated with the task. This can take a while, but subsequent calls will be much faster.
23	
24	<Tip>
25	
26	By default, models will be downloaded from the [Hugging Face Hub](https://huggingface.co/models) and stored in [browser cache](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Cache), but there are ways to specify custom models and cache locations. For more information see [here](./custom_usage).
27	
28	</Tip>
29	
30	You can now use the classifier on your target text by calling it as a function:
31	
32	```javascript
33	const result = await classifier('I love transformers!');
34	// [{'label': 'POSITIVE', 'score': 0.9998}]
35	```
36	
37	If you have multiple inputs, you can pass them as an array:
38	
39	```javascript
40	const result = await classifier(['I love transformers!', 'I hate transformers!']);
41	// [{'label': 'POSITIVE', 'score': 0.9998}, {'label': 'NEGATIVE', 'score': 0.9982}]
42	```
43	
44	You can also specify a different model to use for the pipeline by passing it as the second argument to the `pipeline()` function. For example, to use a different model for sentiment analysis (like one trained to predict sentiment of a review as a number of stars between 1 and 5), you can do:
45	
46	<!-- TODO: REPLACE 'nlptown/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment' with 'nlptown/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment'-->
47	
48	```javascript
49	const reviewer = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis', 'Xenova/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment');
50	
51	const result = await reviewer('The Shawshank Redemption is a true masterpiece of cinema.');
52	// [{label: '5 stars', score: 0.8167929649353027}]
53	```
54	
55	Transformers.js supports loading any model hosted on the Hugging Face Hub, provided it has ONNX weights (located in a subfolder called `onnx`). For more information on how to convert your PyTorch, TensorFlow, or JAX model to ONNX, see the [conversion section](./custom_usage#convert-your-models-to-onnx).
56	
57	The `pipeline()` function is a great way to quickly use a pretrained model for inference, as it takes care of all the preprocessing and postprocessing for you. For example, if you want to perform Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) using OpenAI's Whisper model, you can do:
58	
59	<!-- TODO: Replace 'Xenova/whisper-small.en' with 'openai/whisper-small.en' -->
60	```javascript
61	// Create a pipeline for Automatic Speech Recognition
62	const transcriber = await pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', 'Xenova/whisper-small.en');
63	
64	// Transcribe an audio file, loaded from a URL.
65	const result = await transcriber('https://huggingface.co/datasets/Narsil/asr_dummy/resolve/main/mlk.flac');
66	// {text: ' I have a dream that one day this nation will rise up and live out the true meaning of its creed.'}
67	```
68	
69	## Pipeline options
70	
71	### Loading
72	
73	We offer a variety of options to control how models are loaded from the Hugging Face Hub (or locally).
74	By default, when running in-browser, a *quantized* version of the model is used, which is smaller and faster,
75	but usually less accurate. To override this behaviour (i.e., use the unquantized model), you can use a custom
76	`PretrainedOptions` object as the third parameter to the `pipeline` function:
77	
78	```javascript
79	// Create a pipeline for feature extraction, using the full-precision model (fp32)
80	const pipe = await pipeline('feature-extraction', 'Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2', {
81	    dtype: "fp32",
82	});
83	```
84	Check out the section on [quantization](./guides/dtypes) to learn more.
85	
86	You can also specify which revision of the model to use, by passing a `revision` parameter.
87	Since the Hugging Face Hub uses a git-based versioning system, you can use any valid git revision specifier (e.g., branch name or commit hash).
88	
89	```javascript
90	const transcriber = await pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', 'Xenova/whisper-tiny.en', {
91	    revision: 'output_attentions',
92	});
93	```
94	
95	For the full list of options, check out the [PretrainedOptions](./api/utils/hub#module_utils/hub..PretrainedOptions) documentation.
96	
97	
98	### Running
99	Many pipelines have additional options that you can specify. For example, when using a model that does multilingual translation, you can specify the source and target languages like this:
100	
101	<!-- TODO: Replace 'Xenova/nllb-200-distilled-600M' with 'facebook/nllb-200-distilled-600M' -->
102	```javascript
103	// Create a pipeline for translation
104	const translator = await pipeline('translation', 'Xenova/nllb-200-distilled-600M');
105	
106	// Translate from English to Greek
107	const result = await translator('I like to walk my dog.', {
108	    src_lang: 'eng_Latn',
109	    tgt_lang: 'ell_Grek'
110	});
111	// [ { translation_text: 'Μου αρέσει να περπατάω το σκυλί μου.' } ]
112	
113	// Translate back to English
114	const result2 = await translator(result[0].translation_text, {
115	    src_lang: 'ell_Grek',
116	    tgt_lang: 'eng_Latn'
117	});
118	// [ { translation_text: 'I like to walk my dog.' } ]
119	```
120	
121	When using models that support auto-regressive generation, you can specify generation parameters like the number of new tokens, sampling methods, temperature, repetition penalty, and much more. For a full list of available parameters, see to the [GenerationConfig](./api/utils/generation#module_utils/generation.GenerationConfig) class.
122	
123	For example, to generate a poem using `LaMini-Flan-T5-783M`, you can do: 
124	
125	<!-- TODO: Replace 'Xenova/LaMini-Flan-T5-783M' with 'MBZUAI/LaMini-Flan-T5-783M' -->
126	
127	```javascript
128	// Create a pipeline for text2text-generation
129	const poet = await pipeline('text2text-generation', 'Xenova/LaMini-Flan-T5-783M');
130	const result = await poet('Write me a love poem about cheese.', {
131	    max_new_tokens: 200,
132	    temperature: 0.9,
133	    repetition_penalty: 2.0,
134	    no_repeat_ngram_size: 3,
135	});
136	```
137

1	# The `pipeline` API
2	
3	Just like the [transformers Python library](https://github.com/huggingface/transformers), Transformers.js provides users with a simple way to leverage the power of transformers. The `pipeline()` function is the easiest and fastest way to use a pretrained model for inference. 
4	
5	<Tip>
6	
7	For the full list of available tasks/pipelines, check out [this table](#available-tasks).
8	
9	</Tip>
10	
11	
12	## The basics
13	
14	Start by creating an instance of `pipeline()` and specifying a task you want to use it for. For example, to create a sentiment analysis pipeline, you can do:
15	
16	```javascript
17	import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers';
18	
19	const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis');
20	```
21	
22	When running for the first time, the `pipeline` will download and cache the default pretrained model associated with the task. This can take a while, but subsequent calls will be much faster.
23	
24	<Tip>
25	
26	By default, models will be downloaded from the [Hugging Face Hub](https://huggingface.co/models) and stored in [browser cache](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Cache), but there are ways to specify custom models and cache locations. For more information see [here](./custom_usage).
27	
28	</Tip>
29	
30	You can now use the classifier on your target text by calling it as a function:
31	
32	```javascript
33	const result = await classifier('I love transformers!');
34	// [{'label': 'POSITIVE', 'score': 0.9998}]
35	```
36	
37	If you have multiple inputs, you can pass them as an array:
38	
39	```javascript
40	const result = await classifier(['I love transformers!', 'I hate transformers!']);
41	// [{'label': 'POSITIVE', 'score': 0.9998}, {'label': 'NEGATIVE', 'score': 0.9982}]
42	```
43	
44	You can also specify a different model to use for the pipeline by passing it as the second argument to the `pipeline()` function. For example, to use a different model for sentiment analysis (like one trained to predict sentiment of a review as a number of stars between 1 and 5), you can do:
45	
46	<!-- TODO: REPLACE 'nlptown/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment' with 'nlptown/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment'-->
47	
48	```javascript
49	const reviewer = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis', 'Xenova/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment');
50	
51	const result = await reviewer('The Shawshank Redemption is a true masterpiece of cinema.');
52	// [{label: '5 stars', score: 0.8167929649353027}]
53	```
54	
55	Transformers.js supports loading any model hosted on the Hugging Face Hub, provided it has ONNX weights (located in a subfolder called `onnx`). For more information on how to convert your PyTorch, TensorFlow, or JAX model to ONNX, see the [conversion section](./custom_usage#convert-your-models-to-onnx).
56	
57	The `pipeline()` function is a great way to quickly use a pretrained model for inference, as it takes care of all the preprocessing and postprocessing for you. For example, if you want to perform Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) using OpenAI's Whisper model, you can do:
58	
59	<!-- TODO: Replace 'Xenova/whisper-small.en' with 'openai/whisper-small.en' -->
60	```javascript
61	// Create a pipeline for Automatic Speech Recognition
62	const transcriber = await pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', 'Xenova/whisper-small.en');
63	
64	// Transcribe an audio file, loaded from a URL.
65	const result = await transcriber('https://huggingface.co/datasets/Narsil/asr_dummy/resolve/main/mlk.flac');
66	// {text: ' I have a dream that one day this nation will rise up and live out the true meaning of its creed.'}
67	```
68	
69	## Pipeline options
70	
71	### Loading
72	
73	We offer a variety of options to control how models are loaded from the Hugging Face Hub (or locally).
74	By default, when running in-browser, a *quantized* version of the model is used, which is smaller and faster,
75	but usually less accurate. To override this behaviour (i.e., use the unquantized model), you can use a custom
76	`PretrainedOptions` object as the third parameter to the `pipeline` function:
77	
78	```javascript
79	// Create a pipeline for feature extraction, using the full-precision model (fp32)
80	const pipe = await pipeline('feature-extraction', 'Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2', {
81	    dtype: "fp32",
82	});
83	```
84	Check out the section on [quantization](./guides/dtypes) to learn more.
85	
86	You can also specify which revision of the model to use, by passing a `revision` parameter.
87	Since the Hugging Face Hub uses a git-based versioning system, you can use any valid git revision specifier (e.g., branch name or commit hash).
88	
89	```javascript
90	const transcriber = await pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', 'Xenova/whisper-tiny.en', {
91	    revision: 'output_attentions',
92	});
93	```
94	
95	For the full list of options, check out the [PretrainedOptions](./api/utils/hub#module_utils/hub..PretrainedOptions) documentation.
96	
97	
98	### Running
99	Many pipelines have additional options that you can specify. For example, when using a model that does multilingual translation, you can specify the source and target languages like this:
100	
101	<!-- TODO: Replace 'Xenova/nllb-200-distilled-600M' with 'facebook/nllb-200-distilled-600M' -->
102	```javascript
103	// Create a pipeline for translation
104	const translator = await pipeline('translation', 'Xenova/nllb-200-distilled-600M');
105	
106	// Translate from English to Greek
107	const result = await translator('I like to walk my dog.', {
108	    src_lang: 'eng_Latn',
109	    tgt_lang: 'ell_Grek'
110	});
111	// [ { translation_text: 'Μου αρέσει να περπατάω το σκυλί μου.' } ]
112	
113	// Translate back to English
114	const result2 = await translator(result[0].translation_text, {
115	    src_lang: 'ell_Grek',
116	    tgt_lang: 'eng_Latn'
117	});
118	// [ { translation_text: 'I like to walk my dog.' } ]
119	```
120	
121	When using models that support auto-regressive generation, you can specify generation parameters like the number of new tokens, sampling methods, temperature, repetition penalty, and much more. For a full list of available parameters, see to the [GenerationConfig](./api/utils/generation#module_utils/generation.GenerationConfig) class.
122	
123	For example, to generate a poem using `LaMini-Flan-T5-783M`, you can do: 
124	
125	<!-- TODO: Replace 'Xenova/LaMini-Flan-T5-783M' with 'MBZUAI/LaMini-Flan-T5-783M' -->
126	
127	```javascript
128	// Create a pipeline for text2text-generation
129	const poet = await pipeline('text2text-generation', 'Xenova/LaMini-Flan-T5-783M');
130	const result = await poet('Write me a love poem about cheese.', {
131	    max_new_tokens: 200,
132	    temperature: 0.9,
133	    repetition_penalty: 2.0,
134	    no_repeat_ngram_size: 3,
135	});
136	```
137	
138	Logging `result[0].generated_text` to the console gives:
139	
140	```
141	Cheese, oh cheese! You're the perfect comfort food.
142	Your texture so smooth and creamy you can never get old.
143	With every bite it melts in your mouth like buttery delights
144	that make me feel right at home with this sweet treat of mine. 
145	
146	From classic to bold flavor combinations,
147	I love how versatile you are as an ingredient too?
148	Cheddar is my go-to for any occasion or mood; 
149	It adds depth and richness without being overpowering its taste buds alone
150	```
151	
152	### Streaming
153	
154	Some pipelines such as `text-generation` or `automatic-speech-recognition` support streaming output. This is achieved using the `TextStreamer` class. For example, when using a chat model like `Qwen2.5-Coder-0.5B-Instruct`, you can specify a callback function that will be called with each generated token text (if unset, new tokens will be printed to the console).
155	
156	```js
157	import { pipeline, TextStreamer } from "@huggingface/transformers";
158	
159	// Create a text generation pipeline
160	const generator = await pipeline(
161	  "text-generation",
162	  "onnx-community/Qwen2.5-Coder-0.5B-Instruct",
163	  { dtype: "q4" },
164	);
165	
166	// Define the list of messages
167	const messages = [
168	  { role: "system", content: "You are a helpful assistant." },
169	  { role: "user", content:  "Write a quick sort algorithm." },
170	];
171	
172	// Create text streamer
173	const streamer = new TextStreamer(generator.tokenizer, {
174	  skip_prompt: true,
175	  // Optionally, do something with the text (e.g., write to a textbox)
176	  // callback_function: (text) => { /* Do something with text */ },
177	})
178	
179	// Generate a response
180	const result = await generator(messages, { max_new_tokens: 512, do_sample: false, streamer });
181	```
182	
183	Logging `result[0].generated_text` to the console gives:
184	
185	
186	<details>
187	<summary>Click to view the console output</summary>
188	<pre>
189	Here's a simple implementation of the quick sort algorithm in Python:
190	```python
191	def quick_sort(arr):
192	    if len(arr) <= 1:
193	        return arr
194	    pivot = arr[len(arr) // 2]
195	    left = [x for x in arr if x < pivot]
196	    middle = [x for x in arr if x == pivot]
197	    right = [x for x in arr if x > pivot]
198	    return quick_sort(left) + middle + quick_sort(right)
199	# Example usage:
200	arr = [3, 6, 8, 10, 1, 2]

## Summary

I've thoroughly explored the Transformers.js library and found all the key files and patterns for understanding how pipelines are exported and configured. Here's a comprehensive breakdown:

### 1. **Main Index/Exports File**
- **Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775480750986-g2lkmg/src/transformers.js`
- This file exports everything from `pipelines.js`, `models.js`, `tokenizers.js`, `configs.js`, and utilities
- It's the single entry point for the library; everything exported is public API

### 2. **Pipeline Exports & Registration**
- **Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775480750986-g2lkmg/src/pipelines.js` (3,580 lines)

The pipeline system has three key components:

**a) Pipeline Classes**: Each pipeline is a class that extends `Pipeline`
- Base class at line 164: `export class Pipeline extends Callable`
- Examples: `TextClassificationPipeline`, `ImageToTextPipeline`, `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`
- All export at the class level, e.g., `export class ImageToTextPipeline extends ...`

**b) SUPPORTED_TASKS Object** (lines 3096-3390)
This is the critical configuration that maps task names to their components:

```javascript
"image-to-text": {
    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": ImageToTextPipeline,
    "model": AutoModelForVision2Seq,
    "processor": AutoProcessor,
    "default": {
        "model": "Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning",
    },
    "type": "multimodal",
},
```

Each entry includes:
- `pipeline`: The pipeline class to instantiate
- `model`: The AutoModel class (or array of classes for fallbacks)
- `tokenizer`: Optional tokenizer class
- `processor`: Optional processor class
- `default`: Default model ID from HuggingFace Hub
- `type`: One of `"text"`, `"multimodal"`, `"image"`, `"audio"`

**c) TASK_ALIASES Object** (lines 3394-3403)
Maps user-friendly aliases to canonical task names:
```javascript
"sentiment-analysis": "text-classification",
"ner": "token-classification",
"asr": "automatic-speech-recognition",
"text-to-speech": "text-to-audio",
```

### 3. **Pipeline Factory Function**
- **Location**: Lines 3446-3513 in `pipelines.js`
- **Function**: `export async function pipeline(task, model = null, options = {})`

The factory function:
1. Resolves aliases via `TASK_ALIASES`
2. Looks up task in `SUPPORTED_TASKS`
3. Loads model, tokenizer, and processor in parallel via `loadItems()`
4. Instantiates the pipeline class with loaded components
5. Returns the pipeline instance

### 4. **Task Types by Modality**

From the SUPPORTED_TASKS object, here's how different types are registered:

**Text-only (type: "text")**:
- text-classification, token-classification, question-answering, fill-mask, summarization, translation, text2text-generation, text-generation, zero-shot-classification, feature-extraction, text-to-audio

**Image-only (type: "image")**:
- background-removal, image-to-image, depth-estimation

**Audio-only (type: "audio")**:
- audio-classification

**Multimodal (type: "multimodal")**:
- zero-shot-audio-classification, automatic-speech-recognition, image-to-text, image-classification, image-segmentation, zero-shot-image-classification, object-detection, zero-shot-object-detection, document-question-answering

### 5. **Key Constructor Types**

Pipelines support different constructor patterns via TypeScript JSDoc:
- `TextPipelineConstructorArgs`: Model + tokenizer
- `ImagePipelineConstructorArgs`: Model + processor
- `AudioPipelineConstructorArgs`: Model + processor
- `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs`: Model + tokenizer + processor
- `TextAudioPipelineConstructorArgs`: Model + tokenizer + processor

### 6. **Pipeline Classes Available**
Line 272+ exports these pipeline classes:
- `TextClassificationPipeline`
- `TokenClassificationPipeline`
- `QuestionAnsweringPipeline`
- `FillMaskPipeline`
- `Text2TextGenerationPipeline`
- `SummarizationPipeline`
- `TranslationPipeline`
- `TextGenerationPipeline`
- `ZeroShotClassificationPipeline`
- `FeatureExtractionPipeline`
- `ImageFeatureExtractionPipeline`
- `AudioClassificationPipeline`
- `ZeroShotAudioClassificationPipeline`
- `AutomaticSpeechRecognitionPipeline`
- `ImageToTextPipeline`
- `ImageClassificationPipeline`
- `ImageSegmentationPipeline`
- `BackgroundRemovalPipeline`
- `ZeroShotImageClassificationPipeline`
- `ObjectDetectionPipeline`
- `ZeroShotObjectDetectionPipeline`
- `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`
- `TextToAudioPipeline`
- `ImageToImagePipeline`
- `DepthEstimationPipeline`

### 7. **Model Types Already Available**

From `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775480750986-g2lkmg/src/models.js`:

The model types include `AutoModelForImageTextToText` and `AutoModelForAudioTextToText` which are already defined, indicating infrastructure is ready for these pipelines!

### 8. **Tests Structure**
- **Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775480750986-g2lkmg/tests/pipelines/`
- Individual test files per pipeline: `test_pipelines_image_to_text.js`, `test_pipelines_document_question_answering.js`, etc.
- Pattern: Create pipeline, run tests with batched and non-batched inputs, verify output format, dispose

Example test structure:
```javascript
const PIPELINE_ID = "image-to-text";
let pipe = await pipeline(PIPELINE_ID, model_id, DEFAULT_MODEL_OPTIONS);
// Test single input and batch inputs
await pipe.dispose();
```

### 9. **Package Configuration**
- **Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775480750986-g2lkmg/package.json`
- Main entry: `./src/transformers.js`
- Types entry: `./types/transformers.d.ts` (generated at build time)
- The types file is auto-generated via `npm run typegen` which runs TypeScript

### 10. **Documentation**
- **Snippets**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775480750986-g2lkmg/docs/snippets/5_supported-tasks.snippet`
- **Markdown**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775480750986-g2lkmg/docs/source/pipelines.md`
- Documents all supported tasks with examples and links

---

## For Your `image-text-to-text` Pipeline

Based on this analysis, you'll need to register in these locations:

1. **Create `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class** in `src/pipelines.js`
   - Extend `Pipeline` with `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` 
   - Implement `_call()` method handling both images and text
   - Add JSDoc types for input/output

2. **Add to SUPPORTED_TASKS** in `src/pipelines.js` at line 3390:
   ```javascript
   "image-text-to-text": {
       "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
       "pipeline": ImageTextToTextPipeline,
       "model": AutoModelForImageTextToText,  // Already exists!
       "processor": AutoProcessor,
       "default": {
           "model": "...", // your model ID
       },
       "type": "multimodal",
   }
   ```

3. **Export the pipeline class** automatically via `export class ImageTextToTextPipeline extends ...` in `src/pipelines.js`

4. **Create test file** at `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_text_to_text.js`

5. **Update documentation** in `docs/snippets/5_supported-tasks.snippet` and `docs/source/pipelines.md` (optional but recommended)

6. **Update TypeScript types** will be auto-generated on build via `npm run typegen`

7. **Ensure AutoModelForImageTextToText is exported** from `src/models.js` (already exists based on my grep results)

## Comprehensive Analysis: Transformers.js Model Organization & AutoModelForImageTextToText

Based on my thorough exploration of the Transformers.js codebase, here's a detailed report addressing all your questions:

---

### **1. AutoModelForImageTextToText: EXISTS AND IS FULLY DEFINED**

**Location**: `/src/models.js` (line 8736)

```javascript
export class AutoModelForImageTextToText extends PretrainedMixin {
    static MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS = [MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES];
}
```

**Supported Vision-Language Models** (as of current version):
- `llava` → `LlavaForConditionalGeneration`
- `llava_onevision` → `LlavaOnevisionForConditionalGeneration`
- `moondream1` → `Moondream1ForConditionalGeneration`
- `florence2` → `Florence2ForConditionalGeneration`
- `qwen2-vl` → `Qwen2VLForConditionalGeneration`
- `idefics3` → `Idefics3ForConditionalGeneration`
- `smolvlm` → `SmolVLMForConditionalGeneration`
- `paligemma` → `PaliGemmaForConditionalGeneration`
- `llava_qwen2` → `LlavaQwen2ForCausalLM`
- `gemma3n` → `Gemma3nForConditionalGeneration`
- `mistral3` → `Mistral3ForConditionalGeneration`

---

### **2. Model Auto-Mapping Structure**

The model auto-mapping system is organized through **PretrainedMixin** (line 7843), which follows this pattern:

**Key Files**:
- `/src/models.js` - Core model mappings and definitions
- `/src/models/auto/processing_auto.js` - AutoProcessor for auto-selecting processor classes
- `/src/models/auto/image_processing_auto.js` - Image processor selection
- `/src/models/auto/feature_extraction_auto.js` - Feature extractor selection

**Mapping Structure** (lines 8240-8252):
```javascript
const MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES = new Map([
    ['llava', ['LlavaForConditionalGeneration', LlavaForConditionalGeneration]],
    // ... more models
]);
```

**Type System** (lines 126-141):
```javascript
const MODEL_TYPES = {
    EncoderOnly: 0,
    EncoderDecoder: 1,
    Seq2Seq: 2,
    Vision2Seq: 3,
    DecoderOnly: 4,
    MaskGeneration: 5,
    ImageTextToText: 6,  // <-- Your target type
    // ... more types
};
```

**Model Class Type Mapping** (line 8420):
```javascript
[MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES, MODEL_TYPES.ImageTextToText],
```

---

### **3. How `generate()` Works in Text Generation Pipelines**

The `generate()` method is defined in the `PreTrainedModel` class (line 1735) with these key steps:

**Core Generation Flow**:
1. **Validation & Config Preparation** - Merges generation config with defaults
2. **Model Input Preparation** - `_prepare_model_inputs()` formats inputs
3. **Encoder-Decoder Logic** - If applicable, prepares encoder outputs
4. **Decoder Input Preparation** - Sets up input_ids for autoregressive generation
5. **Logits Processing** - Applies temperature, sampling, penalties
6. **Iterative Token Generation** - Loop that:
   - Calls `forward()` to get logits
   - Applies logits processors
   - Samples next token
   - Continues until stopping criteria met

**For ImageTextToText Models Specifically**:
- Uses `imageTextToTextForward()` (line 819) which calls `genericTextToTextForward()`
- This merges image features with text embeddings before decoding
- Uses `multimodal_text_to_text_prepare_inputs_for_generation()` (line 943)

---

### **4. Processor Classes for Multimodal Models**

Processors extend the base `Processor` class from `/src/base/processing_utils.js`:

**Base Processor Structure** (lines 38-173):
```javascript
export class Processor extends Callable {
    static classes = [
        'image_processor_class',
        'tokenizer_class',
        'feature_extractor_class',
    ]
    
    // Components accessed via getters:
    get image_processor() { return this.components.image_processor; }
    get tokenizer() { return this.components.tokenizer; }
    get feature_extractor() { return this.components.feature_extractor; }
}
```

**Example: LLaVA Processor** (`/src/models/llava/processing_llava.js`):
```javascript
export class LlavaProcessor extends Processor {
    static tokenizer_class = AutoTokenizer
    static image_processor_class = AutoImageProcessor
    static uses_processor_config = true;

    async _call(images, text = null, kwargs = {}) {
        // 1. Process images
        const image_inputs = await this.image_processor(images, kwargs);
        
        // 2. Handle text + expand image tokens
        if (text) {
            const num_image_tokens = calculateTokenCount(image_inputs);
            text = text.replace(image_token, image_token.repeat(num_image_tokens));
        }
        
        // 3. Tokenize text
        const text_inputs = text ? this.tokenizer(text, kwargs) : {};
        
        // 4. Merge and return
        return { ...image_inputs, ...text_inputs };
    }
}
```

**Example: PaliGemma Processor** (`/src/models/paligemma/processing_paligemma.js`):
```javascript
export class PaliGemmaProcessor extends Processor {
    static tokenizer_class = AutoTokenizer
    static image_processor_class = AutoImageProcessor
    static uses_processor_config = false;

    async _call(images, text = null, kwargs = {}) {
        // Handles image token expansion and positioning
        // Supports video inputs as well
        return { ...image_inputs, ...text_inputs };
    }
}
```

**Key Pattern**: Processors combine:
- **Image Processor**: Resizes, normalizes images → `pixel_values`
- **Tokenizer**: Encodes text → `input_ids`, `attention_mask`
- **Special Handling**: Manage image tokens in text (e.g., `<image>`, `<|image_pad|>`)

---

### **5. Vision-Language Models Supported**

**Core VLM Models Mapped in Transformers.js**:

| Model Type | Class | Location | Processor |
|---|---|---|---|
| **LLaVA** | `LlavaForConditionalGeneration` | Extends LlavaPreTrainedModel | `LlavaProcessor` |
| **LLaVA OneVision** | `LlavaOnevisionForConditionalGeneration` | Extends LlavaForConditionalGeneration | Inherits LLaVA |
| **Florence2** | `Florence2ForConditionalGeneration` | Encoder-Decoder | `Florence2Processor` |
| **Qwen2-VL** | `Qwen2VLForConditionalGeneration` | Complex 3D RoPE handling | `Qwen2VLProcessor` |
| **PaliGemma** | `PaliGemmaForConditionalGeneration` | Vision encoder + text decoder | `PaliGemmaProcessor` |
| **IDEFICS3** | `Idefics3ForConditionalGeneration` | Multi-image support | `Idefics3Processor` |
| **SmolVLM** | `SmolVLMForConditionalGeneration` | Lightweight variant | `SmolVLMProcessor` |

**Directory Structure**:
```
/src/models/
  ├── llava/
  │   └── processing_llava.js
  ├── llava_onevision/
  │   └── image_processing_llava_onevision.js
  ├── qwen2_vl/
  │   ├── processing_qwen2_vl.js
  │   └── image_processing_qwen2_vl.js
  ├── paligemma/
  │   └── processing_paligemma.js
  ├── florence2/
  │   └── processing_florence2.js
  └── ... more VLM models
```

---

### **6. MODEL_TYPES & MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS Structure**

**How Model Selection Works**:

1. **Config-Based Loading** (`PretrainedMixin.from_pretrained()`, line 7858):
   - Loads `config.json` → gets `model_type`
   - Searches `MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS` for matching type
   - Returns appropriate model class

2. **CLASS_TYPE_MAPPING** (line 8404):
```javascript
const MODEL_CLASS_TYPE_MAPPING = [
    [MODEL_MAPPING_NAMES_ENCODER_ONLY, MODEL_TYPES.EncoderOnly],
    // ...
    [MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES, MODEL_TYPES.ImageTextToText],
    [MODEL_FOR_AUDIO_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES, MODEL_TYPES.AudioTextToText],
    // ...
];
```

3. **Forward Selection** (lines 1026-1074):
   - Based on `MODEL_TYPES`, assigns:
     - `_forward` function (how to compute forward pass)
     - `_prepare_inputs_for_generation` function (how to prepare inputs for .generate())
     - `can_generate` flag

**ImageTextToText Setup**:
```javascript
case MODEL_TYPES.ImageTextToText:
    this.can_generate = true;
    this._forward = imageTextToTextForward;  // Handles pixel_values
    this._prepare_inputs_for_generation = multimodal_text_to_text_prepare_inputs_for_generation;
    break;
```

---

### **7. Generation Flow for Image-Text-to-Text**

**Forward Pass Chain**:
```
generate() 
  ↓
imageTextToTextForward() [line 819]
  ↓
genericTextToTextForward() [line 718]
  ├─ encode_text({ input_ids })  → text embeddings
  ├─ encode_image({ pixel_values })  → image features
  ├─ _merge_input_ids_with_image_features()  → merge embeddings
  └─ decoderForward()  → autoregressive decoding
```

**Example: Merged Inputs Flow**:
```javascript
async function genericTextToTextForward(self, {
    input_ids,
    pixel_values,
    attention_mask,
    // ... other params
}) {
    // Step 1: Get text embeddings
    inputs_embeds = await self.encode_text({ input_ids });
    
    // Step 2: Get image features
    image_features = await encode_function({ pixel_values });
    
    // Step 3: Merge (model-specific)
    ({ inputs_embeds, attention_mask } = merge_function({
        image_features,
        inputs_embeds,
        attention_mask,
    }));
    
    // Step 4: Run decoder
    return await decoderForward(self, { inputs_embeds, attention_mask });
}
```

---

### **Key Files Summary**

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/src/models.js` | Core model classes, mappings, auto-loading logic |
| `/src/models/auto/processing_auto.js` | AutoProcessor selection |
| `/src/models/llava/processing_llava.js` | LLaVA processor (image + text) |
| `/src/models/qwen2_vl/processing_qwen2_vl.js` | Qwen2-VL processor (3D positional encoding) |
| `/src/models/paligemma/processing_paligemma.js` | PaliGemma processor |
| `/src/base/processing_utils.js` | Base Processor class definition |
| `/src/pipelines.js` | High-level pipeline API |

---

### **Ready for Your Pipeline Implementation**

You can now:
1. ✅ Use `AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained()` to load any supported VLM
2. ✅ Use `AutoProcessor.from_pretrained()` to load the matching processor
3. ✅ Call `processor(images, text)` to prepare multimodal inputs
4. ✅ Call `model.generate(model_inputs)` to generate text from image+text

The architecture is fully set up and production-ready!

   - Searches `MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS` for matching type
   - Returns appropriate model class

2. **CLASS_TYPE_MAPPING** (line 8404):
```javascript
const MODEL_CLASS_TYPE_MAPPING = [
    [MODEL_MAPPING_NAMES_ENCODER_ONLY, MODEL_TYPES.EncoderOnly],
    // ...
    [MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES, MODEL_TYPES.ImageTextToText],
    [MODEL_FOR_AUDIO_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES, MODEL_TYPES.AudioTextToText],
    // ...
];
```

3. **Forward Selection** (lines 1026-1074):
   - Based on `MODEL_TYPES`, assigns:
     - `_forward` function (how to compute forward pass)
     - `_prepare_inputs_for_generation` function (how to prepare inputs for .generate())
     - `can_generate` flag

**ImageTextToText Setup**:
```javascript
case MODEL_TYPES.ImageTextToText:
    this.can_generate = true;
    this._forward = imageTextToTextForward;  // Handles pixel_values
    this._prepare_inputs_for_generation = multimodal_text_to_text_prepare_inputs_for_generation;
    break;
```

---

### **7. Generation Flow for Image-Text-to-Text**

**Forward Pass Chain**:
```
generate() 
  ↓
imageTextToTextForward() [line 819]
  ↓
genericTextToTextForward() [line 718]
  ├─ encode_text({ input_ids })  → text embeddings
  ├─ encode_image({ pixel_values })  → image features
  ├─ _merge_input_ids_with_image_features()  → merge embeddings
  └─ decoderForward()  → autoregressive decoding
```

**Example: Merged Inputs Flow**:
```javascript
async function genericTextToTextForward(self, {
    input_ids,
    pixel_values,
    attention_mask,
    // ... other params
}) {
    // Step 1: Get text embeddings
    inputs_embeds = await self.encode_text({ input_ids });
    
    // Step 2: Get image features
    image_features = await encode_function({ pixel_values });
    
    // Step 3: Merge (model-specific)
    ({ inputs_embeds, attention_mask } = merge_function({
        image_features,
        inputs_embeds,
        attention_mask,
    }));
    
    // Step 4: Run decoder
    return await decoderForward(self, { inputs_embeds, attention_mask });
}
```

---

### **Key Files Summary**

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/src/models.js` | Core model classes, mappings, auto-loading logic |
| `/src/models/auto/processing_auto.js` | AutoProcessor selection |
| `/src/models/llava/processing_llava.js` | LLaVA processor (image + text) |
| `/src/models/qwen2_vl/processing_qwen2_vl.js` | Qwen2-VL processor (3D positional encoding) |
| `/src/models/paligemma/processing_paligemma.js` | PaliGemma processor |
| `/src/base/processing_utils.js` | Base Processor class definition |
| `/src/pipelines.js` | High-level pipeline API |

---

### **Ready for Your Pipeline Implementation**

You can now:
1. ✅ Use `AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained()` to load any supported VLM
2. ✅ Use `AutoProcessor.from_pretrained()` to load the matching processor
3. ✅ Call `processor(images, text)` to prepare multimodal inputs
4. ✅ Call `model.generate(model_inputs)` to generate text from image+text

The architecture is fully set up and production-ready!

Now I have all the information I need. Let me now write my comprehensive plan.

Key findings:
1. `AutoModelForImageTextToText` already exists in `src/models.js` (line 8736) and is exported via `export class AutoModelForImageTextToText extends PretrainedMixin`


2. The pipeline class needs to be added to `src/pipelines.js` along with registering the task in `SUPPORTED_TASKS`, and importing it in the factory function via JSDoc comments
3. A test file needs to be created at `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_text_to_text.js`, which will be auto-discovered by the test runner without any additional registration

Now let me think about the implementation. The key difference from `ImageToTextPipeline` is that this pipeline needs to handle both an image and a text prompt together, similar to how `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline` works. The approach is to accept the image and text, process them together through the processor (since many VLM processors like LLaVA and PaliGemma accept both modalities in a single call), then generate outputs using the model and decode them with the tokenizer.

For the `_call` implementation, I'm preparing the images first, then passing both the prepared images and text to the processor to get the combined inputs, which handles the different ways VLM processors might combine image and text data. Continuing with the generation logic...

Finishing the implementation...

Now I'm realizing the processor returns all the necessary inputs like `pixel_values`, `input_ids`, and `attention_mask` that can be passed directly to `model.generate()`, so I just need to merge those with any additional generation parameters and decode the output back to text.

For testing this pipeline, I'll need a small model from the `hf-internal-testing` repository—something like a tiny LLaVA or PaliGemma variant that can handle both image and text inputs. I should check what's available there and note it in the plan.

Defining the output type...

Now I'm thinking through how batching should work for this pipeline. Looking at similar pipelines, `ImageToText` accepts an array of images and returns results, while `DocumentQuestionAnswering` takes a single image with a question. For `image-text-to-text`, I could either accept a single image plus text prompt, or handle batches of images with text—I need to decide which approach makes the most sense.

Since vision language models naturally process image and text together, the simpler approach of accepting `(image, text, generate_kwargs)` where image can be either a single image or an array seems more practical for JavaScript. This keeps the implementation straightforward while still supporting batching when needed.

Now I need to figure out what model to use as the default—something lightweight and well-tested for actual use, but for tests I'll need a tiny random model from the hf-internal-testing namespace. Let me outline the implementation plan, starting with the changes needed in `src/pipelines.js` to add the image-text-to-text pipeline support. I need to add `"image-text-to-text"` to the pipeline function's docstring as well. Looking at how `ImageToTextPipeline` works, it only extracts `pixel_values` from the processor output, but for VLM models like LLaVA and PaliGemma, the processor returns additional fields like `input_ids` and `attention_mask` that need to be passed to the model. So in `ImageTextToTextPipeline._call`, I'll pass the complete processor output when calling the model's generate method.

For decoding the output, I'm checking whether to use `this.tokenizer.batch_decode` or `this.processor.batch_decode`. Looking at the existing pipelines, `AutomaticSpeechRecognitionPipeline` uses the processor while `ImageToTextPipeline` uses the tokenizer directly. Since VLM models have the tokenizer available on the pipeline instance, I'll use `this.tokenizer.batch_decode` for consistency.

On batching, I'm leaning toward supporting only single image-text pairs initially, similar to `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`, even though the processor could technically handle batches. VLMs are typically used for single queries anyway, and I can add batch support later if needed.

Writing the pipeline method...

content.text:
Here is the complete implementation plan:

---

## Plan: `image-text-to-text` Pipeline

### What was found

The entire model infrastructure is already in place — **`AutoModelForImageTextToText`** is defined in `src/models.js` (line 8736) and already supports LLaVA, PaliGemma, SmolVLM, Qwen2-VL, IDEFICS3, Florence2, and more. It's also already re-exported through `src/transformers.js` via `export * from './models.js'`. **All work is isolated to `src/pipelines.js` plus one new test file.**

---

### Changes Required (2 files total)

#### 1. `src/pipelines.js` — 4 targeted additions

**Step 1 — Import:** Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the import block from `./models.js` (line ~44), alongside other Auto* model classes.

**Step 2 — JSDoc typedefs:** Add three standard typedef blocks before the new class:
- `ImageTextToTextSingle` (`{ generated_text: string }`)
- `ImageTextToTextOutput` (array of single)
- `ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback` callback signature `(image, text, options?) → Promise<ImageTextToTextOutput>`
- `ImageTextToTextPipelineType` intersection type

**Step 3 — Class definition:** Insert `ImageTextToTextPipeline` after `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline` (line 2779), before the `VocoderOptions` typedef. The `_call(image, text, generate_kwargs)` method:
1. Calls `prepareImages(image)` (existing utility, line 91)
2. Calls `this.processor(preparedImages, text)` — VLM processors accept image+text together and handle image-token expansion internally
3. Spreads the full `inputs` object into `this.model.generate()` — passes `pixel_values`, `input_ids`, `attention_mask` together (matches the `imageTextToTextForward` function in models.js)
4. Decodes via `this.tokenizer.batch_decode(output, { skip_special_tokens: true })`
5. Returns `[{ generated_text: "..." }]`

**Step 4 — SUPPORTED_TASKS registration:** Add entry after `"image-to-text"` (line 3255):
```js
"image-text-to-text": {
    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": ImageTextToTextPipeline,
    "model": AutoModelForImageTextToText,
    "processor": AutoProcessor,
    "default": { "model": "onnx-community/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct" },
    "type": "multimodal",
}
```

**Step 5 — pipeline() JSDoc:** Add `"image-text-to-text"` to the task list in the factory function's JSDoc (line ~3427).

#### 2. `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_text_to_text.js` — New file

Follows the exact pattern of `test_pipelines_image_to_text.js` and `test_pipelines_document_question_answering.js`. Uses `load_cached_image("white_image")` and a small `hf-internal-testing/tiny-random-LlavaForConditionalGeneration` model. Auto-discovered by `collect_and_execute_pipeline_tests()` — no registration needed.

---

### What does NOT need to change
- `src/transformers.js` — `export * from './pipelines.js'` and `export * from './models.js'` handle everything automatically
- `src/models.js` — `AutoModelForImageTextToText` is already complete
- No existing pipeline class is modified

2732	export class DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
2733	
2734	    /**
2735	     * Create a new DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline.
2736	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
2737	     */
2738	    constructor(options) {
2739	        super(options);
2740	    }
2741	
2742	    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
2743	    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {
2744	
2745	        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1
2746	
2747	        // Preprocess image
2748	        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
2749	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);
2750	
2751	        // Run tokenization
2752	        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
2753	        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
2754	            add_special_tokens: false,
2755	            padding: true,
2756	            truncation: true,
2757	        }).input_ids;
2758	
2759	        // Run model
2760	        const output = await this.model.generate({
2761	            inputs: pixel_values,
2762	            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
2763	            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
2764	            decoder_input_ids,
2765	            ...generate_kwargs,
2766	        });
2767	
2768	        // Decode output
2769	        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];
2770	
2771	        // Parse answer
2772	        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
2773	        let answer = null;
2774	        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
2775	            answer = match[1].trim();
2776	        }
2777	        return [{ answer }];
2778	    }
2779	}
2780	
2781	
2782	/**
2783	 * @typedef {Object} VocoderOptions
2784	 * @property {PreTrainedModel} [vocoder] The vocoder used by the pipeline (if the model uses one). If not provided, use the default HifiGan vocoder.
2785	 * @typedef {TextAudioPipelineConstructorArgs & VocoderOptions} TextToAudioPipelineConstructorArgs
2786	 */
2787	
2788	/**
2789	 * @typedef {Object} TextToAudioOutput
2790	 * @property {Float32Array} audio The generated audio waveform.
2791	 * @property {number} sampling_rate The sampling rate of the generated audio waveform.
2792	 *
2793	 * @typedef {Object} TextToAudioPipelineOptions Parameters specific to text-to-audio pipelines.
2794	 * @property {Tensor|Float32Array|string|URL} [speaker_embeddings=null] The speaker embeddings (if the model requires it).
2795	 * @property {number} [num_inference_steps] The number of denoising steps (if the model supports it).
2796	 * More denoising steps usually lead to higher quality audio but slower inference.
2797	 * @property {number} [speed] The speed of the generated audio (if the model supports it).
2798	 *
2799	 * @callback TextToAudioPipelineCallback Generates speech/audio from the inputs.
2800	 * @param {string|string[]} texts The text(s) to generate.
2801	 * @param {TextToAudioPipelineOptions} options Parameters passed to the model generation/forward method.
2802	 * @returns {Promise<TextToAudioOutput>} An object containing the generated audio and sampling rate.
2803	 *
2804	 * @typedef {TextToAudioPipelineConstructorArgs & TextToAudioPipelineCallback & Disposable} TextToAudioPipelineType
2805	 */
2806	
2807	/**
2808	 * Text-to-audio generation pipeline using any `AutoModelForTextToWaveform` or `AutoModelForTextToSpectrogram`.
2809	 * This pipeline generates an audio file from an input text and optional other conditional inputs.
2810	 *
2811	 * **Example:** Generate audio from text with `onnx-community/Supertonic-TTS-ONNX`.

2009	 * @typedef {Object} ImageTextToTextSingle
2010	 * @property {string} generated_text The generated text.
2011	 * @typedef {ImageTextToTextSingle[]} ImageTextToTextOutput
2012	 *
2013	 * @callback ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback Answer questions about an image.
2014	 * @param {ImageInput} image The input image.
2015	 * @param {string} text The text prompt to condition generation on.
2016	 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
2017	 * @returns {Promise<ImageTextToTextOutput>} An array containing the generated text.
2018	 *
2019	 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageTextToTextPipelineType
2020	 */
2021	
2022	/**
2023	 * Image-text-to-text pipeline using any `AutoModelForImageTextToText`.
2024	 * This pipeline accepts an image and a text prompt and generates text.
2025	 *
2026	 * **Example:** Answer a question about an image with `Xenova/llava-1.5-7b-hf`.
2027	 * ```javascript
2028	 * const pipe = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'Xenova/llava-1.5-7b-hf');
2029	 * const image = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
2030	 * const prompt = 'What animals are in this image?';
2031	 * const output = await pipe(image, prompt);
2032	 * // [{ generated_text: 'There are cats in the image.' }]
2033	 * ```
2034	 */
2035	export class ImageTextToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageTextToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
2036	
2037	    /**
2038	     * Create a new ImageTextToTextPipeline.
2039	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
2040	     */
2041	    constructor(options) {
2042	        super(options);
2043	    }
2044	
2045	    /** @type {ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback} */
2046	    async _call(image, text, generate_kwargs = {}) {
2047	
2048	        // Preprocess image
2049	        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
2050	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);
2051	
2052	        // Tokenize text prompt
2053	        const text_inputs = this.tokenizer(text, {
2054	            padding: true,
2055	            truncation: true,
2056	        });
2057	
2058	        // Generate output token ids
2059	        const outputTokenIds = /** @type {Tensor} */ (await this.model.generate({
2060	            inputs: text_inputs.input_ids,
2061	            pixel_values,
2062	            attention_mask: text_inputs.attention_mask,
2063	            ...generate_kwargs,
2064	        }));
2065	
2066	        // Decode output tokens to text
2067	        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(outputTokenIds, {
2068	            skip_special_tokens: true,
2069	        });
2070	
2071	        return decoded.map(generated_text => ({ generated_text: generated_text.trim() }));
2072	    }
2073	}
2074	
2075	/**
2076	 * @typedef {Object} ImageClassificationSingle
2077	 * @property {string} label The label identified by the model.
2078	 * @property {number} score The score attributed by the model for that label.
2079	 * @typedef {ImageClassificationSingle[]} ImageClassificationOutput
2080	 *
2081	 * @typedef {Object} ImageClassificationPipelineOptions Parameters specific to image classification pipelines.
2082	 * @property {number} [top_k=1] The number of top labels that will be returned by the pipeline.
2083	 *
2084	 * @callback ImageClassificationPipelineCallback Assign labels to the image(s) passed as inputs.
2085	 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images The input images(s) to be classified.
2086	 * @param {ImageClassificationPipelineOptions} [options] The options to use for image classification.
